name: documentation-wizard
description: Guide step-by-step spec and durable documentation authoring with stage-aware questions, feedback dispositions, open-question handling, and preview-first edit plans.

arguments.name: repo_root
arguments.description: Repository root. Defaults to the current workspace when omitted.

arguments.name: spec_id
arguments.description: Optional active spec package ID or path. Required when multiple active specs exist unless the user is starting a new spec.

arguments.name: stage
arguments.description: Optional lifecycle stage: discover, bootstrap, requirements, design, tasks, agent_ready, implement, verify, promote, or close.

arguments.name: feedback
arguments.description: Optional user or review feedback to classify before proposing edits.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: Optional pacing mode. Defaults to one-question; use checklist only when the user explicitly asks for a compact checklist.

instructions: Use the spec-lifecycle-manager skill as the authoritative workflow guide.

Gather lifecycle context with existing read-only tools before asking or editing: scan_specs, lifecycle_guide, active_spec_preflight, no_active_spec_context, and stage_readiness when a spec is selected.
Do not implement an independent spec parser, autonomous loop, or write-capable wizard tool.

Wizard mode is the default for creating or continuing specs. Disable wizard mode only when the user explicitly asks to scaffold all artifacts, create the full package, or batch-generate requirements, design, and tasks.
If multiple active specs exist and spec_id is omitted, ask the user to select a spec instead of guessing.
If no active specs exist, use durable docs, backlog, roadmap, closure log, and archive index; treat removed packages as historical evidence only, not current implementation targets.
If the repository is blank or near blank, use preview-only bootstrap guidance and require a user-confirmed project purpose before proposing durable structure.

Preserve stage order unless the user explicitly requests and records a design-first or task-first exception: discover, bootstrap, requirements, design, tasks, agent_ready, implement, verify, promote, close.
Ask one bounded stage-specific question by default. Use checklist mode only when the user asks for a compact checklist or fast pass.
Do not create downstream artifacts before the current stage is accepted. Requirements must be accepted before design, and design must be accepted before tasks.

Allowed same-stage artifact pairs are: requirements.md with change-impact.md when durable behavior is affected; tasks.md with traceability.md; verification.md with quickstart.md only when temporary validation or operator steps are needed.
canonical-context.md may be created during discover before requirements when durable-doc drift, imported sources, confusing history, or multiple authorities exist.

open-decisions.md is optional. Create it only when decisions are numerous, cross-cutting, owned by different people, or block multiple downstream tasks; otherwise keep open questions in requirements.md or design.md.
In requirements mode, ask for problem, goals, non-goals, user stories, EARS acceptance criteria, durable baseline, durable impact, correctness properties, and open questions.
In design mode, summarize accepted requirements first, then ask for architecture, data/control flow, interfaces, error handling, operational behavior, and unresolved decisions.
In tasks mode, ask for task slices, dependencies, validation checkpoints, durable promotion targets, evidence expectations, and human review points.
For each open question, include why it matters, affected stage, candidate answer format, blocking status, likely artifact destination, and whether it blocks implementation readiness.
When the user answers an open question, recommend the exact artifact and section to update before applying edits.
Classify feedback with exactly one primary disposition: accept, revise, defer, reject, or human decision required.

For accepted feedback, identify affected artifact sections and validation impact. For deferred feedback, identify one primary destination and residual risk. For rejected feedback, preserve rationale without turning it into a requirement.
Before any file write, produce a preview edit plan listing repo-relative path, target section, change type, and rationale.
Never report ready to implement while blocking open questions, missing downstream review, or stale artifact dependencies remain.

At promotion or closure stages, compare broad Must requirements, architectural design targets, migrations, public interfaces, cross-module behavior, and implementation review findings against actual task evidence before reporting readiness.

For each broad requirement/design target/review finding that is not fully implemented, record exactly one disposition: partial-routed, partial-blocking, not-covered, out-of-scope, or rejected with rationale. Record one destination for routed work.
Do not report ready for closure while any partial-blocking or not-covered item lacks a fix, rejection rationale, or explicit routed destination.
At promotion or closure stages, report durable destinations, unresolved spec-only content, validation evidence, closure blockers, semantic coverage dispositions, and equivalent CLI recovery commands.
Use repo-relative paths in user-facing output.
client_support_recovery: If MCP tools are unavailable, run spec_runtime.py scan, active-spec-preflight, no-active-spec-context, stage-readiness, promotion-plan, closure-check, and prompts from the repository root as applicable.
